why_this_action.summary: The execution board was still truthfully empty inside the short-window hold, so the strongest move available was to harden the live directory-proof lane itself. The shared backlink tracker was missing already-live secondary SaaSHub surfaces, which hid a real Codeberg-vs-GitHub routing problem on third-party pages.
shared_findings_used: agents/marketing/logs/adoption_metrics_latest.json: Codeberg is still flat and remains the primary success gate
agents/marketing/logs/distribution_lane_latest.json: current lane is a hold-state guard with no truthful do-now packet
agents/marketing/logs/marketing_workflow_audit_latest.json: another same-family publisher/contact burst would blur measurement
drafts/marketing_execution_board_latest.md: no truthful do-now handoff packet exists in the current review window
agents/marketing/logs/backlink_status_latest.json: only 2 direct live listings were being counted before this repair
agents/marketing/logs/market_intelligence_latest.json: reuse shared proof surfaces instead of inventing another siloed packet

expected_outcome: Future directory-confirmation and lane-selection work can now see that SaaSHub has extra live Ralph Workflow surfaces and that at least one of them still routes repo intent to GitHub-only. That gives the next non-fake directory follow-through a concrete Codeberg-routing repair target instead of treating SaaSHub as already fully solved.
